summary: Iteration 34 — Multi-branch CRUD + geofence in /attendance/punch + OCR ID proof (Gemini 3 Flash) VERIFIED end-to-end. Backend pytest 14/14 PASS (with one server bug found + fixed). Frontend Playwright mobile 390x844: Branches CRUD flow (add/edit/delete) works from company_admin Home Quick Action; KYC screen shows all 4 scan chips; OCR endpoint invoked from browser context reaches backend and correctly surfaces 502 (not 500) on Gemini failure.

minor.issue: Server was returning 500 for the first create because `db.branches.insert_one(doc)` mutates `doc` in-place to add MongoDB `_id` (ObjectId), then `return {"branch": doc}` triggered `TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable` inside FastAPI's jsonable_encoder. Fixed in this iteration by popping `_id` before return (server.py L2716-L2718). Recommend consistent `.find_one(..., {'_id': 0})` after insert across all similar create endpoints.

When Gemini returns a BadRequestError (e.g. tiny/invalid image), backend correctly raises HTTPException(502) per spec — NOT a 500. However Cloudflare (K8s ingress edge) intercepts backend 5xx and rewrites the response body to an HTML error page. Direct backend contract is correct; the HTML wrap is an infra concern only. Consider catching Gemini BadRequest specifically and returning a 200 with ok=false so the client always sees JSON.

ui_bugs.issue: ActionRow 'Branches' on Home (index.tsx L481-486) has no testID. Currently selected by visible text 'Branches'. Suggest adding testID='row-branches' for stable selection.

action_items: Consider adding testID='row-branches' to the Home ActionRow so tests don't rely on visible text.

OPTIONAL: When Gemini raises BadRequestError (e.g. unreadable/tiny image), map to 200 {ok:false, detail:'...'} instead of 502 so clients receive JSON at the ingress layer (Cloudflare rewrites 5xx to HTML in this preview env).
OPTIONAL: audit all `insert_one(doc) → return {...doc}` sites for the same ObjectId-serialization pattern (fix applied only in create_branch this iter).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L2716: create_branch returned the just-inserted doc without stripping the `_id` that insert_one mutated in — this made the API 500 on any create. Fixed.

server.py L1145-1173: OCR endpoint correctly wraps Gemini exceptions in a 502 (per iter-34 spec). Consider narrowing to `except (BadRequestError, TimeoutError)` and returning a 200 with ok=false for BadRequestError so the mobile app never has to parse HTML from a 502 gateway page.
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral only. Backend suite creates 2 companies + 4 users + 4 sessions (+ branches/attendance during tests) — all cleaned in module teardown. Frontend suite created 1 company + 2 users (company_admin + employee) + 2 sessions (tag IT34F2b11c6) — all cleaned post-run via pymongo. Verified 0 leftover IT34* rows in branches/users/companies/user_sessions/attendance.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The OCR /me/ocr-id-proof endpoint returns real Gemini responses via emergentintegrations. Tiny/blank JPEGs return 502 (as designed). Use a real photo of an Aadhaar/PAN card to see 200 with parsed fields. Also, the K8s ingress (Cloudflare) rewrites all backend 5xx to HTML — assert only status code, not JSON body, for 502 paths.
rca_of_issue: POST /api/company/branches returned 500 because Motor's insert_one() adds a Mongo `_id: ObjectId` to the input dict in place. The endpoint then returned that same dict inside the response, and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder raised `TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable`. Repro: any first-time create call would 500. Fix: pop `_id` from `doc` before returning (or use find_one with projection). Verified fix by re-running pytest → all 14 pass. Frontend UI walk now also completes save→edit→delete without error.
super_admin_verification: Real super_admin (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com) doc post-run: role=super_admin, pin_must_change=True, pin_fail_count=2 (unchanged from pre-run state — PIN NOT touched).
